segments.text: Then Jesus entered Jericho and was passing through.
And there was a man named Zacchaeus, a chief tax collector, who was very wealthy.
He was trying to see who Jesus was, but could not see over the crowd because he was small in stature.
So he ran on ahead and climbed a sycamore tree to see Him, since Jesus was about to pass that way.
When Jesus came to that place, He looked up and said,
Zacchaeus, hurry down, for I must stay at your house today.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So Zacchaeus hurried down and welcomed Him joyfully.
And all who saw this began to grumble, saying,
He has gone to be the guest of a sinful man!
segments.speaker: Crowd

segments.text: But Zacchaeus stood up and said to the Lord,
Look, Lord, half of my possessions I give to the poor, and if I have cheated anyone, I will repay it fourfold.
segments.speaker: Zacchæus

segments.text: Jesus said to him,
Today salvation has come to this house, because this man too is a son of Abraham.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the Son of Man came to seek and to save the lost.
segments.speaker: Jesus

text: The Parable of the Ten Minas

segments.text: While the people were listening to this, Jesus proceeded to tell them a parable, because He was near Jerusalem and they thought the kingdom of God would appear imminently.
So He said,
A man of noble birth went to a distant country to lay claim to his kingship and then return.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Beforehand, he called ten of his servants and gave them ten minas.‘Conduct business with this until I return,’ he said.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But his subjects hated him and sent a delegation after him to say, ‘We do not want this man to rule over us.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When he returned from procuring his kingship, he summoned the servants to whom he had given the money, to find out what each one had earned.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The first servant came forward and said, ‘Master, your mina has produced ten more minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His master replied, ‘Well done, good servant! Because you have been faithful in a very small matter, you shall have authority over ten cities.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The second servant came and said, ‘Master, your mina has made five minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And to this one he said, ‘You shall have authority over five cities.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then another servant came and said, ‘Master, here is your mina, which I have laid away in a piece of cloth.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man. You withdraw what you did not deposit and reap what you did not sow.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: His master replied, ‘You wicked servant, I will judge you by your own words. So you knew that I am a harsh man, withdrawing what I did not deposit and reaping what I did not sow?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why then did you not deposit my money in the bank, and upon my return I could have collected it with interest?’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then he told those standing by, ‘Take the mina from him and give it to the one who has ten minas.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: ‘Master,’ they said, ‘he already has ten!’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He replied, ‘I tell you that everyone who has will be given more; but the one who does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: And these enemies of mine who were unwilling for me to rule over them, bring them here and slay them in front of me.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: After Jesus had said this, He went on ahead, going up to Jerusalem.
As He approached Bethphage and Bethany at the Mount of Olives, He sent out two of His disciples,
saying,
Go into the village ahead of you, and as you enter it, you will find a colt tied there, on which no one has ever sat. Untie it and bring it here.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: If anyone asks, ‘Why are you untying it?’ tell him, ‘The Lord needs it.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So those who were sent went out and found it just as Jesus had told them.
As they were untying the colt, its owners asked,
Why are you untying the colt?
segments.speaker: Colt Owners

segments.text: The Lord needs it,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they answered.
Then they led the colt to Jesus, threw their cloaks over it, and put Jesus on it.
As He rode along, the people spread their cloaks on the road.
And as He approached the descent from the Mount of Olives, the whole multitude of disciples began to praise God joyfully in a loud voice for all the miracles they had seen:
Blessed is the King who comes in the name of the Lord!
segments.speaker: Disciples

segments.text: Peace in heaven and glory in the highest!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: But some of the Pharisees in the crowd said to Him,
Teacher, rebuke Your disciples!
segments.speaker: Pharisees

segments.text: I tell you,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He answered,
if they remain silent, the very stones will cry out.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: As Jesus approached Jerusalem and saw the city, He wept over it
and said,
If only you had known on this day what would bring you peace! But now it is hidden from your eyes.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For the days will come upon you when your enemies will barricade you and surround you and hem you in on every side.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will level you to the ground—you and the children within your walls. They will not leave one stone on another, because you did not recognize the time of your visitation from God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus entered the temple courts and began to drive out those who were selling there.
He declared to them,
It is written: ‘My house will be a house of prayer.’ But you have made it ‘a den of robbers.’
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus was teaching at the temple every day, but the chief priests, scribes, and leaders of the people were intent on killing Him.
Yet they could not find a way to do so, because all the people hung on His words.
